That commit includes the Linux testbed CMake/preset wiring, the Linux case-sensitive asset-cooker path fix, and the current `.cozter` snapshot. The change set had already been validated before publish with `cmake --preset linux-clang-testbed-x64`, `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-testbed-dbg --parallel 8`, and a direct `resource_cooker` run, all successful.
even after I reloaded the cmake in CLion, the testbed still marked as unavailable

I’m checking whether this is still a CMake graph issue or just a CLion profile mismatch. First I’m inspecting the current Linux presets and the `testbed` target wiring as imported today, then I’ll confirm which configure presets actually define `testbed` and whether the default Linux profile still excludes it.

I found the concrete CLion-side cause. Your local workspace still has the `testbed` run configuration bound to `windows-clang-testbed-x64 - windows-clang-testbed-dbg`, and the Linux-specific testbed profiles are imported but disabled in `.idea/workspace.xml`. That is why CLion keeps labeling it unavailable on Linux even though the target now exists in CMake.

[Tool Result]
$ /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '32,62p' .idea/workspace.xml" (exit 0)

I’m making two fixes now. First, I’m correcting the repo preset so the normal Linux “full” profile actually includes `testbed`, matching Windows. Second, I’m correcting your local CLion workspace so the existing `testbed` run configuration points at a Linux profile instead of the stale Windows one.

[File Change]

The issue was not the `testbed` target itself anymore. It was a profile mismatch.

I fixed the repo preset so the normal Linux full profile now includes `testbed` in [CMakePresets.json:105](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/CMakePresets.json#L105), and I fixed your local CLion workspace so the Linux testbed profiles are enabled in [.idea/workspace.xml:48](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/.idea/workspace.xml#L48) and the `testbed` run configuration now points at `linux-clang-x64 - linux-clang-dbg` in [.idea/workspace.xml:208](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/.idea/workspace.xml#L208).

I verified it from the same profile CLion was using:
`cmake --preset linux-clang-x64` now reports `Linux targets enabled: frame, loader, testbed, logserver, resource_cooker`
and `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-dbg --target testbed --parallel 8` succeeds.

If CLion still shows the old state, it is likely holding the stale workspace in memory. Reload the changed files or restart CLion once; after that the existing `testbed` run configuration should stop being marked unavailable.

This is not committed yet. Current local changes are `.cozter`, [CMakePresets.json](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/CMakePresets.json), and [.idea/workspace.xml](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/.idea/workspace.xml).

also, can you check the mesh shader and computer shader backend?
cus, when it renders the cube, it shows that 8 vertices are connected, but the indices order looks wrong. it doesn't look like a cube

I’m checking the mesh-shader path and the compute-emulation path side by side, starting from the cube asset data through the generated index buffers. The first pass is to locate where the cube topology is authored and where each backend turns that into primitives, so I can tell whether this is bad source data, bad backend translation, or just wrong winding/order on one path.

The authored cube triangles look sane. I’m now checking the lower-level data layout, because both shader-driven backends read the same vertex/index buffers and the current GLSL side declares the source vertex buffer as a `std430` struct array. If that layout does not match the packed 24-byte CPU vertex data, both backends would generate the same wrong topology even with correct indices.

The next check is whether the backend actually carries a structured stride into Vulkan descriptors. If it does not, then the current GLSL `std430` struct array is definitely reading a different memory layout than the 24-byte packed geometry buffer, which would explain why both the mesh path and compute-emulation path are wrong in the same way.

I found the likely shared bug rather than a triangle-order bug. Both backends consume the geometry vertex buffer through `mesh_shader_authoring.glsli`, and that shader currently interprets the source vertices as a `std430` array of `{ vec3 position; vec3 color; }`. Your CPU geometry buffer is packed at 24 bytes per vertex, but `std430` will pad that GLSL struct to 32 bytes, so both backends are reading the wrong bytes for every vertex after the first one.

I found a concrete backend bug and I’m fixing that rather than touching the cube indices. The shader-driven vertex buffer is authored as packed `position + color` floats with a 24-byte stride, but the GLSL mesh/compute path is currently reading it as a `std430` struct array, which pads each source vertex to 32 bytes. I’m changing the shared mesh-shader authoring include to decode the packed float layout explicitly so both the mesh backend and the compute-emulation backend read the same source geometry correctly.

I checked both the mesh-shader path and the compute-emulation path. The cube indices in [cube.nwb](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/Testbed/assets/meshes/cube.nwb#L17) are fine; the bug was lower-level. Both backends were reading the source vertex buffer through [mesh_shader_authoring.glsli](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/assets/graphics/mesh_shader_authoring.glsli#L29) as a `std430` array of `{ vec3 position; vec3 color; }`, but the CPU uploads that geometry as a tightly packed 24-byte vertex stream in [renderer_system.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/ecs_graphics/renderer_system.cpp#L355) and [cube.nwb](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/Testbed/assets/meshes/cube.nwb#L3). In GLSL, that struct gets padded to 32 bytes, so both backends were reading the wrong bytes after the first vertex.

I fixed the shared backend code in [mesh_shader_authoring.glsli](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/assets/graphics/mesh_shader_authoring.glsli#L33) and [mesh_shader_authoring.glsli](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/assets/graphics/mesh_shader_authoring.glsli#L86) to decode the packed float layout explicitly. That change applies to both the real mesh shader and the compute-emulation backend, so they now consume the same source geometry correctly.

I forced fresh shader recooks for both Linux output trees, and both completed successfully:
`Graphics asset cook complete [dbg] - files=16`
I could not visually inspect the rendered window from this environment, so you should rerun the testbed once locally. Current uncommitted changes are [.cozter/sessions/c93aeca0e5be.json](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/.cozter/sessions/c93aeca0e5be.json), [.idea/workspace.xml](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/.idea/workspace.xml), and [mesh_shader_authoring.glsli](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/assets/graphics/mesh_shader_authoring.glsli). If the cube looks right now, I can commit and push this fix.

summary:
**Context Block**
- Repo: `NWBLot` at `/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot`; branch `main`; `gh` CLI still unavailable, so publish flow uses plain `git`.
- Standing user rule remains: always include `.cozter` changes in future publishes for this repo.
- Prior baseline (already done before this segment): Linux CMake wiring/style updates and earlier commits (`69ad33e`, `a0bfb1c`, `641603c`, `b9bf130`, `e81f3d0`, `e51effc`, `09d8f7d`, `4fd40d8`) were already pushed; only `main`/`origin/main` branches remained after cleanup.

- Publish completed from pending Linux testbed/path-case work:
- Commit `59813f6` pushed to `origin/main` with message `Enable Linux testbed and preserve cook paths`.
- Included 6 files: `.cozter/sessions/c93aeca0e5be.json`, `CMakeLists.txt`, `CMakePresets.json`, `core/assets/asset_cooker.h`, `impl/assets_graphics/shader_asset_cooker.cpp`, `resource_cooker/resource_cooker_command_line.cpp`.
- Tool results: `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/main...HEAD` moved `0 1` (pre-push) then `0 0` (post-push); `git status` clean immediately after push.

- CLion “testbed unavailable” follow-up:
- Root cause found: profile mismatch in local `.idea/workspace.xml` (testbed run config bound to Windows profile; Linux testbed profiles disabled), plus standard Linux preset behavior mismatch.
- `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-dbg --target help` initially lacked usable `testbed` in the profile CLion used; `linux-clang-testbed-dbg` had it.
- Fixes applied during troubleshooting: edited `CMakePresets.json` (normal Linux full profile includes testbed) and local `.idea/workspace.xml` (enabled Linux testbed profile + remapped `testbed` run config to `linux-clang-x64 - linux-clang-dbg`).
- Validation: `cmake --preset linux-clang-x64` succeeded; `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-dbg --target help` showed `testbed`; `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-dbg --target testbed --parallel 8` succeeded.
- This CLion workspace file remained local/uncommitted.

- Shader-language migration request (“use GLSL only”) completed and published:
- Decision: keep stage contracts (`ps`/`vs`) unchanged; convert source/includes + metadata from HLSL/DXC to GLSL/glslang; remove HLSL acceptance in Vulkan cook path.
- Files changed for migration:
- `Testbed/assets/shaders/bxdf_ps.nwb` (`compiler="glslang"`, profile `ps`).
- `impl/assets/graphics/mesh_emulation_vs.nwb` (`compiler="glslang"`, profile `vs`).
- `core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_shader_compiler.cpp` (reject HLSL/DXC source path).
- HLSL assets replaced by GLSL equivalents: `Testbed/assets/shaders/bxdf_ps.glsl`, `Testbed/assets/shaders/bxdf.glsli`, `impl/assets/graphics/cube_ps.glsli`, `impl/assets/graphics/mesh_emulation_vs.glsl`; old `.hlsl/.hlsli` removed/renamed.
- Validation results: both `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-dbg --target testbed --parallel 8` and `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-testbed-dbg --target testbed --parallel 8` succeeded; repo-wide `rg` (excluding third-party/build dirs) found no remaining `.hlsl/.hlsli/dxc/hlsl` references.
- Commit `051f062` pushed to `origin/main` with message `Convert shaders to GLSL`; included `.cozter` and shader migration set; `.idea/workspace.xml` intentionally left unstaged.
- Post-push checks: `git rev-list ...` = `0 0`; `HEAD`/`origin/main` at `051f062`.

- Latest task: mesh shader + compute-emulation backend cube topology issue investigation:
- Finding: not cube index order; cube indices in `Testbed/assets/meshes/cube.nwb` are sane.
- Root cause: shared shader include interpreted source vertex buffer via `std430` struct (`vec3 position + vec3 color`), which pads to 32 bytes; actual CPU geometry stream is tightly packed 24-byte stride (`vertex_stride = 24`), causing bad vertex reads in both mesh and compute-emulation paths.
- Fix applied in `impl/assets/graphics/mesh_shader_authoring.glsli`: switched to explicit packed-float decoding logic for source vertices (affects both backends) and added a guard comment to prevent layout-regression.
- Build tool results: incremental builds reported `ninja: no work to do`, so cooker was forced manually.
- Forced recook commands run from the correct executable directories for both trees (`testbed` and `full`), both succeeded with `Graphics asset cook complete [dbg] - files=16`.
- Visual runtime verification not possible in this environment; user still needs to run testbed locally to confirm cube now renders correctly.

- Current working state now:
- Branch still `main`, synced with `origin/main` (no new commit after `051f062`).
- Dirty worktree currently contains exactly:
- `.cozter/sessions/c93aeca0e5be.json`
- `.idea/workspace.xml`
- `impl/assets/graphics/mesh_shader_authoring.glsli`
- Open commitment: if user confirms visual fix, commit/push mesh backend fix on `main`, including `.cozter` and excluding `.idea/workspace.xml` unless explicitly requested.
